title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends
Energy and shipping organizations have met to outline plans to accelerate the development of a green corridor to transport iron ore between Australia and East Asia. Read More

A week is an eternity in geopolitics
The drone attacks on Russian naval assets in Crimea last weekend led to the suspension of the country's participation in the deal that has allowed Ukraine to recommence seaborne exports of grains. Read More

No coal was imported from the Philippines, South Africa or Colombia
Coal import data for September by origin has been released and shows that imports from Indonesia have remained China's primary source and Russia the second largest source.

children.summary: In the second week of November, the downward trend in freight rates has not yet reversed, with the number of ships sailing ballast gradually increasing, while demand (ton days) is growing even more slowly.

children.summary: This insight explores the sharp climb in Iran's crude/condensate exports observed in October, changes to floating storage and Russia's impact on Iranian fleet activity.

children.title: The Big Picture: Supply forecast
children.summary: Ordering this year has naturally slowed down as the freight market softened throughout the year.

children.title: The weakness has come as a surprise
section_path: The weakness has come as a surprise
children.section_path_text: The weakness has come as a surprise
children.summary: As we discussed in our most recent Weekly Dry Bulk Report, India produced only 109.6 billion kilowatt hours of electricity last month which is down month-on-month by 10.4 billion kilowatt hours (-9%) and down year-on-year by 1.2 billion kilowatt hours (-1%).
